ecosystem: open country / thermals / ridges / agricultural areas
season: summer (migratory; Mar/Apr to Sep/Oct in PNW)

taxonomy.family_relationship: NOT related to Old World vultures (Accipitridae); convergent evolution. More closely related to storks than to hawks.

vocalization.mechanism: no syrinx; esophageal air forcing produces hiss and grunt
vocalization.frequency_hz: broadband 200-2000 Hz hiss; no structured vocalization
vocalization.energy_placement: E=2 -- near-minimum. Bird-level record low in engine.

special_ability.olfaction: Detects dimethyl sulfide (DMS) at 1 part per billion -- produced in first hours of animal death. Olfactory bulb 4x larger relative to body size than hawks.
special_ability.thermal_soaring: V-wing dihedral. Metabolic rate barely above resting in thermal flight. 100-200km per day on minimal energy.

threats: lead poisoning from ammunition in gut piles

theory_nodes.concept: FIRST CATHARTIDAE. New World vultures enter engine. Cathartidae = convergent evolution with Old World vultures (Accipitridae). Seven species: Turkey Vulture, Black Vulture, California Condor, Andean Condor, three others.
theory_nodes.description: Cathartidae enters the engine at degree 235.

theory_nodes.concept: WIDEST S36/SPS E-GAP IN ENGINE. Turkey Vulture (E=2) + Heart Hard Rock (E=7) = E-spread of 5. Record: no prior degree exceeds 4. Maximum vocal contrast.
theory_nodes.description: Degree 235 holds the engine's widest S36/SPS energy gap.

theory_nodes.concept: No syrinx. The Turkey Vulture is the first bird in the engine with no functional syrinx. E=2 reflects biological reality: esophageal air = hiss and grunt, not bird song.
theory_nodes.description: The physiological basis of E=2 -- the absent syrinx.

cross_references.relationship: Falconidae closes at 234. Cathartidae opens at 235. Immediate family transition: the falcon-to-vulture boundary.
FIRST FISH follows FIRST CATHARTIDAE. Two sequential family milestones. Engine expands rapidly at 235-236.

s36_pair.pairing_note: WIDEST E-GAP IN ENGINE. E=2 + E=7 = spread of 5. Near-silent bird + maximum female hard rock. Both underestimated by cultural reception; both more powerful than their reception suggests.
